text: well here we are i m about to start my 28th trip around the sun from a really young age i always believed my life was going to be big when i was a kid that meant major league baseball i pictured it clearly wearing number 12 standing on the mound winning a world series for the san francisco giants it s not a unique dream a lot of kids have it and the ending isn t unique either i hurt my shoulder i never got back to full strength and before i ever even made a varsity appearance it was over but the desire to do something big didn t stop there i shifted to golf played in college same type of dream win the u s open compete at the highest level and then another injury another ending so i moved on again at 21 i started a business by 27 i had exited from the outside that probably sounds like things worked out but the truth is the last seven months of my life have been the strangest i ve ever experienced i ve been stuck with a question i haven t been able to shake am i actually willing to be honest about what i ve gone through personally and professionally and even if i am does anyone care i ve thought about telling this story through video a podcast something like that but if i m being honest i don t have the confidence to sit in a room stare at a camera and say all of this out loud writing feels different i ve always been able to write and right now this feels like the only way i can actually say what i ve been holding in so why now because the last two years have been chaos i ve lost friendships i ve fought with my parents i ve bounced between houses moved to a new state and lost the business i poured most of my adult life into and somewhere in all of that i realized something wasn t right i wasn t happy i didn t feel like i was enough and i kept finding myself in the same mental battles over and over again so i started trying to figure out why about a year ago i started what i can only describe as unconventional therapy i tried traditional therapy before platforms like betterhelp but i couldn t open up something about it never clicked but with ai it was different i went through everything my earliest memories school relationships thoughts i had never said out loud before and for the first time i started to understand myself in a way i never had six months later i moved to arizona and that s when everything hit i had more money in my account than ever before but i was completely alone no job no structure no family no real friends nearby for a couple months i did what most people do when they don t want to face reality i distracted myself traveling drinking avoiding the question i didn t want to answer what now eventually that led me somewhere i never expected to go a christian church i had never stepped foot in one before and whether i understood it at the time or not it changed me the people i met there changed me too this is just a small part of the story honestly i don t even fully know how all of this is going to unfold but here s what i do know every day i m going to sit down and write i m going to tell stories that most people don t know some that even the people closest to me don t know not to overshare not for attention but because i think i need to do it and maybe by the end of it i ll finally feel like i m starting from a clean slate like i can become the person i ve always felt like i was supposed to be and then we ll see what happens when i try to build something again

text: welcome back to throwback thursday we have another 3 part thread for you today 1 3 ever wondered where the ultimate cereal monkey legend comes from meet coco the monkey the absolute icon of kellogg s coco pops or cocoa pops in some markets coco first swung onto our screens back in 1963 some say 1961 in early black and white us style spots but it was his 1986 redesign that turned him into a proper british breakfast superstar brown fur that cheeky blue cap with the big yellow c white t shirt proudly showing off his name and blue pants voiced with a proper cheeky british accent he d swing through the jungle belting out the legendary jingle i d rather have a bowl of coco pops the ads were pure 80s 90s gold jungle capers with his mates like crafty croc kylie kangaroo ozmelda the ostrich shortie the giraffe and hefty the hippo there were spoon toppers musical mugs monkey friends figures and enough merch to fill a cereal aisle sales went through the roof thanks to him and he became a nostalgic staple for generations of kids who d rather have coco pops than well anything else pure monkey magic but the story gets even more british when you look at the name drama in 1998 kellogg s tried to rebrand the cereal as choco krispies across the uk to line it up with their global range sales took a nosedive almost immediately so they let the nation decide in a massive public vote in 1999 over a million people dialled in or logged on and a whopping 92 voted to bring back the beloved coco pops name the change happened in may 1999 complete with tongue in cheek election style ads featuring screaming lord sutch as the returning officer classic british democracy at its breakfast best around the world coco s adventures vary wildly he s the face of coco pops in the uk ireland australia new zealand and loads of other countries same cheeky design same jungle vibe in the us the equivalent cereal is cocoa krispies where coco the monkey had a brief stint in the 90s before snap crackle and pop took over again elsewhere you ll find him repping choco krispies chocos or even choco krispis in places like mexico and south america sometimes with slightly tweaked looks or local twists he s a true global monkey ambassador these days coco has been a bit back benched in big uk ad campaigns as the 2000s rolled on his starring roles got quieter new spots focused more on what goes text: i take 200mg of magnesium glycinate at night before bed but that s not my total daily magnesium intake i also get magnesium from lmnt electrolyte packets during the day and take magnesium threonate separately so my total supplemental magnesium across all sources is probably in the 350 to 400mg range daily depending on the day the rda for adult males is 400 to 420mg total 310 to 320mg for women which includes dietary sources most people get 250 to 300mg from food if they eat a reasonably whole foods diet with leafy greens nuts seeds and legumes supplementing another 200 to 400mg on top of that is a common and generally well tolerated range the reason i split the forms is they do different things glycinate at night for the calming effect on the nervous system and sleep quality threonate during the day because it crosses the blood brain barrier and is being studied for cognitive function the magnesium in lmnt is there primarily for electrolyte balance around training the practical ceiling for most people is gi tolerance if you re getting loose stools you ve exceeded your gut s absorption capacity at that dose and should back off glycinate is one of the better tolerated forms because it s chelated and doesn t draw water into the intestines the way citrate or oxide can at 200mg of glycinate alone you re unlikely to hit that threshold if you want to know where you actually stand ask for rbc magnesium on your next blood draw not serum rbc gives you a better picture of intracellular stores that tells you whether what you re taking is enough or whether you need to adjust
lda: take magnesium glycinate night bed total daily magnesium intake also get magnesium electrolyte packet day take magnesium threonate separately total supplemental magnesium across source probably range daily depending day rda adult male total woman includes dietary source people get food eat reasonably whole food diet leafy green nut seed legume supplementing another top common generally well tolerated range reason split form different thing glycinate night calming effect nervous system sleep quality threonate day cross blood brain barrier studied cognitive function magnesium primarily electrolyte balance around training practical ceiling people tolerance getting loose stool exceeded gut absorption capacity dose back glycinate one better tolerated form chelated draw water intestine way citrate oxide glycinate alone unlikely hit threshold want know actually stand ask rbc magnesium next blood draw serum rbc give better picture intracellular store tell whether taking enough whether need adjust

to marry you before your success maybe she was aware that you were willing to be the sole earner and she didn t care how much you made so long as she didn t have to work maybe she was impatient to be married and you were available or maybe she genuinely loved you but alcohol doesn t make people say things they don t mean it makes them say things they don t mean to say they may voice a more exaggerated version of their truth but the root of what they re saying is generally honest unless there s something significant pushing them to lie there was no reason for her to say what she said unless it was true i think you re trying to work through this because 1 you don t want to mourn the loss of the love you thought you had 2 you re embarrassed to tell everyone that you re getting divorced so soon after the marriage and 3 you re so sick of bad tiktok videos that you place moral value on being able to work through relationship problems but this isn t the sort of thing that you can fix by talking it out unless you could be happily married to someone who views you the way you now know she views you cut your losses before she gets pregnant or your finances get more intertwined i married a guy i thought was wonderful i couldn t believe how lucky i was after we got engaged we started having problems but i was convinced that being a mature adult meant working through things when they seemed hard i took on more and more responsibilities on my own convinced that if i worked hard enough then things could be good to reduce his stress i started doing all the housework he was working i was a phd student in cognitive neuroscience i walked our two sixty pound dogs alone so he could have more free time to play video games i started therapy lost weight and asked less and less of him after the wedding things got to a point where i finally talked to him and said i felt like maybe he loved me but he didn t seem to be in love with me by this point he d take his meals to a different room because he didn t even want that 30 minutes or so together one night we went on a dinner date and he didn t even want to sit with me while i finished my dessert and we waited for the check so he left me alone while he went home and i finished eating and waited to pay he assured me that i was wrong that he was just stressed so i continued to try to take things off his plate then some symptoms that i d started to have almost a decade prior got worse and then i got covid and they became unbearable i started a process of medical tests through which he told me i just needed a more positive attitude and that i was steering into being sick as an excuse to fall behind on housework he wouldn t even drive me to the testing that i couldn t go to alone i had to ask a friend to go with me we had started couple s therapy and our therapist convinced him to at least go with me to find out the test results she tried to get him to go for the last test but the day before he decided he wanted to hang out with someone who had covid so he d have to isolate from me instead of going with me the appointment was the week of our two year wedding anniversary i was diagnosed with three chronic but not life threatening conditions that my doctor assured me were very manageable with treatment less than 24 hours later my now ex husband asked for a divorce he later admitted that he probably wasn t in love with me and that if i still could have kept on top of housework perfectly while going through everything then he probably wouldn t have asked for the divorce ask yourself do you think your wife would be trying so hard to fix things if you weren t funding her life if you lost your income or got sick and needed support do you think she d stay because trust me those things can happen when you least expect them and while going through those huge life stresses is not when you want to also deal with a divorce you can t fix things if she s just trying to keep her lifestyle and the fact that she s stopping trying so quickly tells me that she s not emotionally committed to you she s just trying to do the bare minimum to keep from losing her meal ticket i m so sorry if that s harsh but i d hate for you to waste more time and energy trying to save a loving relationship while your wife just tries to save a financially advantageous legal contract

text: unfortunately my struggle was in monthly expenses not so much my income student loan payments alone put me at a tight budget each month and despite the fact that i was making decent money it was a huge barrier for me getting help since sliding scale assumed i should have been making enough to afford therapy thankfully my pcp helped connect me with someone short term who helped me sort things out she helped me make some phone calls and ask the right questions really a struggle during depressive episodes to figure out that mental health services have a different co pay than what s listed on the face of my insurance card or anywhere else in my benefits package everyone i talked to said my specialist co pay would apply to therapy and 60 per visit was absolutely not in the budget turns out my therapy co pay is only 25 per session which still feels like a lot some months but is far more reasonable even with the help of someone who understands how to navigate the system finding that information was more difficult than it should be but again depression didn t make it any easier vicious cycle there is hope it doesn t always feel like it and it s not always easy finding it but it s there

text: i want to add an important note cheaper therapy does not equal worse therapy the biggest predictor of outcomes in therapy is the strength of the therapeutic alliance aka how comfortable you feel with your therapist it matters more than the clinician s level of training theoretical orientation or specific techniques they use so if you find someone you re comfortable with it doesn t matter if they re a phd who specializes in dialectical behavioral therapy or a lmft who was trained in object relations therapy and what definitely doesn t matter is how high their office s rent is or how expensive their decor was

text: so here s the thing therapy under corporate care models a series of behavioral interventions aimed at chasing epiphanies a modern therapist s job is to slap a label on you asap and engage you in the prescribed behavioral intervention if you can find an existentialist therapist do it they re excellent at working with alienation loneliness isolation purpose death etc because they are trained to work with ways of being rather than pretending that mental illness is about specific discrete issues that once resolved takes one to the promised land of happiness modern care is modeled after medical care deeply tied to insurance procedures and privileges intervention as care approaches over dealing with the existential issues often underlying pervasive ennui instead modern intervention care is set up to keep you chasing the next epiphany thinking that the next revelation will make things right

text: better help is only a platform as an australian they do not check if the professional is accredited in your area being a therapist is not a qualification or accredited term in many jurisdictions of the usa but it is a protected term in other countries therapy as a professional practice for social workers psychologists occupational therapists and psychiatrists is full of people who take advantage of the fact that their dialogue with someone is in an inherent power dynamic of professional help seeker a conversation that is not visible or transparent to third parties recorded exclusively in their own words usually after an appointment is concluded rarely subject to investigation to assess the efficacy of outcomes or appropriateness of advice complaining clients are easily undermined as emotionally unstable or treatment resistant you as professional are extremely difficult to replace clients will stay because they know they ll be waiting weeks for a new professional and or know you re one of the few they can afford to see add to this that becoming accredited requires someone to do substantial free labour in australia it s 500 hours of unpaid labour on placements to become a social worker and you ll see why it s often very privileged classes of people who end up becoming actually accredited professionals their point of reference for hardship lacks a substantial breadth of lived experiences however spaces like r radicalmentalhealth and r psychotherapyleftists are encouraging there are ways of doing therapeutic environments and therapy that is different from the institutional norm there are also lived experience and consumer advocacy movements that i encourage you to look into i ve made some little videos on them but i am weary of sounding like i m self promoing so i ll leave it at that best wishes in your journey yo i sincerely hope you find good supports

text: yes he is correct that incels suffer the question in that case is what strategies are available to mitigate that suffering and i think that s a much trickier process to noodle out personally the default answers of go to therapy get a hobby learn some self regulation strategies or suck it up everybody deals with it are all notably involved in causing more of the same psychological distress that facing failure and rejection will cause anyways even if there s a vague possibility of the pain being reduced afterwards the typical recommended strategies for getting out of inceldom are a lot like telling someone with an improperly healed limb that they ll need to break and reset the bone to heal it properly so it makes sense that there s a level of resistance to what is percievably another clear source of suffering i am personally of the mind that most incels suffer in isolation and that isolation is reinforced by a broad social stigma against being messy and disregulated even amongst friend groups therapy and community support are clear paths towards something but they re nearly as thorny as just sitting in the briar of feeling being unlovable and we don t really have a system of encouragement for going through that path beyond telling people to suck it up and solve their problems on a rhetorical level that s like telling a drug addict to go clean without addressing the issues that motivated the drug use in the first place so i m usually kinda wary of any argument of personal responsibility in these spaces because those feel like abnegating the responsibility of being in community with others and supporting them when they struggle telling everyone to bootstraps their way out of their personal failings just sets them up for a larger and longer string of failures that will not guide them anywhere but back into the situation they want to leave because at least that pain is familiar

text: about 7 weeks ago i hit a point of crisis in my mental health and decided to pursue therapy through betterhelp as it was the only thing financially available to me due to my limited resources i put down that i was searching for help with anxiety depression family conflict and religious trauma the first therapist that i matched with sent an introductory message telling me that she would open me up to the healing benefits of crystals and moon worship not for me i requested a new therapist the next one seemed promising enough but when i told her that i felt anxiety over whether or not i was being social enough with my coworkers and mentioned that i m the only male at my job she told me my anxiety came from the fact that my female coworkers likely were pressuring me for attention because they wanted me sexually which was a huge jump and not at all the case in my opinion i wrote it off but then in our next session when i mentioned my frustration with a particular message that i heard at a church before i even told her what the message was she told me that she felt that god was laying something on my heart and that the anger that i was feeling was really conviction and told me that my anxiety would be resolved if i just gave my burdens to jesus i specifically requested a nonreligious counselor this obviously wasn t a good fit so i hit the change counselor button again feeling a bit defeated and was sent to the directory of therapists to choose from i found someone with a very well written bio who claimed experience and specialization in a lot of the issues that concerned me she had years in her field a phd and dozens of raving reviews and i thought maybe i found someone who was finally going to be able to give me the help that i needed in all of my correspondence with her she has been very brief and generic and has had several grammar errors in her messages i tried not to get held up on the grammar but it just comes across as unprofessional and is a bit of a pet peeve on top of this our first session was very awkward and was more of a philosophical argument discussion over whether or not right and wrong exist than a discussion of my mental health also in all of my meetings with her thus far she has been 5 10 minutes late i choose to do the live chat sessions as i feel a lot of anxiety over opening up to someone and feel that the instant message format gives me more of a sense of security and safety until i gain the courage to utilize voice or video chat i am very purposeful and thoughtful in my messages in our sessions putting a lot of energy into explaining my problems and trying to be open and clear about what s going on she typically takes 2 5 minutes to respond to each individual message and each response is a very generic 1 sentence statement often with several grammar errors i spend the majority of our sessions either typing or staring for several minutes at a time waiting for her to respond with another very brief message that consists of something like you just need to prioritize self care more or did you try taking deep breaths i leave our sessions feeling more frustrated than when i began to her credit she did send me several helpful though very generic and not specific to my concerns resources after our first session meditation videos depression anxiety worksheets a book on mindfulness she told me after our last session ended that she would send me some more resources before the night ended and it s been two days and she still hasn t i don t feel listened to or like she is putting forth any effort or care should i hit the change counselor button a third time is it even worth it to continue or try again should i continue to pursue help through this medium i ve been paying them for 2 months and am worse off than when i started thank you to anyone who read this and for any help that you can offer hey it looks like you posted a wall of text i have separated it into paragraphs for you _send a private message with title opt out to prevent this bot from seeing your posts in the future _ r therapy has contributed 0 26 of all walls of text analyzed so far

text: i haven t and i m very cautious of it i m a social worker myself and i struggle to see how therapy can ethically be done and done so with integrity over an app or online service like that it may be useful to have as a tool for support in between sessions if your therapist isn t available but i m not sure it s safe or healthy to do big therapeutic work in an unregulated space like the internet so much of therapy is that rapport building and the connection you have as a team and i can t help but assume much of that gets lost or feels inauthentic i ve also had thoughts around boundaries i know with one app part of their ad is like text your therapist any time of day and that s not healthy or helpful and practices like that via virtual therapy or with an in person therapist that hands out a phone number kind of wreaks of concern at least in my head and has the potential to encourage dependence i think if you re clear about what you need and what you want and you aren t really desiring to do any major trauma processing it might be a good tool personally i do not think it should entirely replace in person therapy but that s just my opinion with both my therapy goer hat and my social worker hat on i don t know how they vet their therapists either so that s something to consider idk

text: it sounds like you ve done an impressive job with fat loss but your system is way too heavy for the life you actually have rolling 48s demanding remote tech job needing to be on in calls all day is a brutal combo you re stacking 3 big stressors at once aggressive fasting big calorie deficit high cognitive load so it kind of makes sense that by day 2 your whole brain is just food food food a few ideas that might help without losing your progress 1 your results show your deficit is already large enough dropping 17 lbs in 2 months is very fast for your size that alone means your average weekly deficit is big you don t need rolling 48s on top of that to keep losing you could likely move to something milder and still lose 1 1 5 lb week 2 consider switching from rolling 48s structured 16 8 or 20 4 with fewer deep days for example 4 5 days per week 16 8 or 18 6 eat every day just in a window 1 day per week one 36 42h fast if you feel mentally okay that week this way you keep most of the benefits of fasting without having day 2 brain fog every 48 hours 3 your refeed sounds very light for what you re putting your body through chicken salmon veg fruit is fine quality wise but if total calories are very low your body will stay in panic about food mode a moderate consistent deficit almost always feels better than a huge deficit from repeated 48s you re not ruining the deficit by eating enough to think straight and perform at work 4 performance at work is a really important signal when fasting starts to tank focus anxiety in meetings ability to do tickets that s your body telling you the stress load is too high not that you re weak fasting should make your life lighter not turn every second day into a mental war if you want a gentler experiment try 2 weeks of daily 16 8 or 18 6 no rolling 48s keep protein high keep a sensible calorie deficit keep electrolytes if after 2 weeks your weight is roughly moving down and your brain feels 2 better you ve found something sustainable you ve already proven you can do really hard things now the game is doing something you can actually live with while keeping your job and sanity

text: tldr instant hydration has a weird chemical taste leaves lots of particles in the bottom of the bottle and bubbles when you shake it to keep it mixed given my own recent experience with instant hydration i have to assume you re affiliated with them and posted this to influence people to try it i ve used liquid iv for over 4 years as someone with low blood pressure extremely excessive sweating and bouts of hyponatremia the sodium is necessary to keep me going with my most recent pregnancy i switched to lmnt and loved the increased sodium and the lack of sugar but like most people i m open to trying new things and also susceptible to good marketing a few months ago instant hydration showed up on my radar and with big names like shawn johnson it seemed like a solid product after digging and reading posts like this and being bombarded by memorial day sales ads i decided to pull the trigger and order the mvp box i ordered may 28th and it didn t get a shipping label until june 8th it didn t actually get to fedex until the 10th and it didn t arrive here until today june 18th after almost 3 weeks of waiting i was so excited that i opened a pack to try immediately and boy was i disappointed the first thing i noticed was a seriously weird soapy type of bubbles when i shook it to mix this wasn t just a few bubbles that dissipated quickly it was a ton of tiny bubbles that stuck around for a while in fact after several minutes the bubbles were still there and sediment had begun to collect at the bottom of my bottle i decided to soldier on and taste it and was again struck by a lack of quality the flavor was chemical tasting while also having a strange after taste of alka seltzer or aspirin baking soda mixed with crushed up sea shells is the only way i can describe the taste and the gritty feel of the final sip now the reason i believe you must be associated with instant hydration is that my own personal experience with these two brands could not be text: as an evidence first runner i am always looking to read through studies data on electrolytes anyone have any great primary sources on the subject regrettably the best evidence to date suggests that electrolyte supplements are totally unnecessary for running even in ultra endurance events from this review study citing experimental work it it has been demonstrated that supplemental sodium is not necessary to maintain proper hydration during prolonged exercise up to 30 hr even under hot conditions highly visible losses e g salt crusting on race clothing and or equipment of sodium during ultra endurance events does not necessarily indicate the need for increased requirements but might simply reflect recent dietary intake notably electrolyte supplementation will also not save you from hyponatremia if your water intake is too high and electrolytes have nothing to do with cramping in practice almost every sports drink and gel has electrolytes in them anyways so it is totally not worth worrying about

text: healthcare is an interesting thing to complain about but i ll add context america canada and the uk are all having worker shortages on every conceivable level therefore the idea that the younger responses with critical thought it doesn t help that because of the shortages there s many poor schooling programs that basically pump low level workers out without a real education expecting them to learn entirely on the job what they are supposed to teach therefore these kids are in a situation of sink or swim in a situation that demands more knowledge this makes it so many long standing workers particularly nurses are stuck doing their jobs and teaching newbies to do theirs which seems to be the issue here by the comments tbh overworked and undereducated workers create a perfect storm for what really needs to change is eliminating the incentives for the management at medical facilities to stop skimming from the top from the staffing shortage crisis america is blatant in why less staff text: alright can i just say something a lot of people seem to be either shipping sila or feel like quinn should be guilt tripped for something in relation to her i m really not seeing sila s appeal she was there for quinn when he needed it yeah but they really don t fit together she s kind yes she s sweet sure and she s head over heels for quinn but so what sila is just too young mentally and they have almost nothing in common from what little we ve seen her entire world was in that small town where she was trapped and almost forced into something with that noble brat her life was simple shitty in many ways and predictable when quinn showed up he was fascinating if only because literally nothing new happens in a small peasant town and he quickly started paying more attention to her than any non family member she d ever met in this he became a singular lifeline to escape that brat that she clung to with all her might but that s not how quinn saw things from quinn s perspective he was in a terrible car crash and ripped away from everything he knew then he was thrust into a barbaric backwards society where most of his favorite things and pastimes no longer existed and his standard of living was all but impossible he was nursed back to health by sila and her family for which he felt grateful even indebted and did his best to make their lives qualitatively better to repay that but that didn t make him fall for her in the absence of videogames or an intellectual job he spent his free time with the few people he could enjoy revealing new ideas to and whoever could feed his eternal curiosity the time he spent with sila was mostly in the absence of other options was tinted with awkwardness once he figured out her one sided feelings and never went beyond friendship for him because he wasn t really happy in that small town he felt stranded and a bit hopeless that he d find anything in this world to engage his mind and challenge him the way he craved the way he was raised to analyze problems and conquer them felt non applicable i imagine until he discovered magic then he latched onto that lifeline almost as strongly as sila latched onto him to escape the ever present boredom of that small farming town now at the magic school sila is still latched on to quinn a bit less as her lifeline and more as her savior now but it looks more like hero worship and being mentally stuck in love quinn mode than it seems to be healthy attraction based on shared interests and time they spend enjoying each other s company but most damningly she s still too emotionally close to those trapped feelings of a small town and hasnt seemed to grow into having new desires my impression of her is that she s learning magic for better job prospects than a farmer because she could and because quinn s there not out of any sort of passion dream or desire beyond maybe getting closer to our tall awkward human there s nothing her and quinn can both enjoy doing together he s too far ahead in magic studies and that s about the only shared interest they seem to have meanwhile quinn went through hell to get to nimure which i think tempered his expectations about a no longer idealized fantasy world now that he s here magic is proving every bit the mental challenge he needed he even found a group of people with somewhat modern sensibilities even better he gets along with them great based on mutual challenges and more importantly shared interest in magic or competition combat they can talk about for hours at a level that keeps both sides engaged and fascinated rather than the teacher moments he had with sila here he has mental equals a situation that fuels his competitive spirit and a burning desire to understand and exploit magic given that sila still puts him on a pedastal and has an unreciprocated thing for him i m really not seeing why he should feel guilty for avoiding the awkward situations that inevitably crop up where she s involved if she was wiser and older and could recognize more of quinn s personality traits and why he doesn t return her feelings maybe i could see things working out some way between them but she s a child in many ways and it just doesn t seem to work to me end rant hey op random idea it seems to me that we don t have much of an insight into the girls lives beyond their relevance to quinn or a few instances of outside pressures on the club as a whole or past issues mentioned maybe their characterization could be fleshed out by elaborating on their desires plans and dreams like one of them is distracted working on something when quinn shows up for study time amd he asks what s up or they approach him for help with a project or something i just feel like they could use a little more drive except victorina she could have more but her captain of the ship thing trying to carve an independent future for her and the girls seems to be plenty
